title: p-Adic Analytic Continuation of Genus 2 Overconvergent Hilbert Eigenforms in the Inert Case
speaker: Yichao Tian
affiliation: Princeton University; Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: A well known result of Coleman says that p-adic overconvergent (ellitpic) eigenforms of small slope are actually classical modular forms. Now consider an overconvergent p-adic Hilbert eigenform F for a totally real field L. When p is totally split in L, Sasaki has proved a similar result on the classicality of F. In this talk, I will explain how to treat the case when L is a quadratic real field and p is inert in L.

proof_stages.label: Coleman analytic-continuation template
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls Coleman's small-slope classicality theorem for overconvergent p-adic modular forms, Kassaei's analytic-continuation proof via f=a_p^{-1}U_pf, and related generalizations as the motivating model for the inert Hilbert modular case.

proof_stages.label: Hilbert modular surfaces and overconvergent forms
proof_stages.summary: For a real quadratic field in which p is inert, the speaker defines the Hilbert modular varieties X and Y with Gamma_{00}(N) and Gamma_0(p) level, the ordinary locus and its canonical section, the weight line bundles, the classical and overconvergent spaces, and the natural injection from classical to overconvergent forms.

proof_stages.label: Small-slope theorem and reduction to extension
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem states that a U_p-eigen overconvergent Hilbert modular form of weight (k_1,k_2) and slope less than min(k_1,k_2)-2 is classical, and Koecher-principle plus rigid-GAGA arguments reduce this to extending the form to the tube of an open special-fiber subset whose complement has codimension two.

proof_stages.label: U_p correspondence and partial-degree coordinates
proof_stages.summary: The U_p operator is realized by a Hecke correspondence parametrizing an additional OF-stable cyclic subgroup H' distinct from H, and partial degrees of finite flat OF-stable group schemes give coordinates on Y^rig by the square [0,1]^2, with the ordinary locus at (1,1).

proof_stages.label: Boundary target and canonical-locus continuation
proof_stages.summary: Since the boundary of the partial-degree square corresponds to the required codimension-two extension target, the proof extends f from a strict ordinary neighborhood first over the canonical locus using known U_p dynamics, then outlines extensions to the special boundary strata, to the positive-degree region, and finally gluing near the totally degree-zero locus.

proof_stages.label: Partial Hasse invariant analysis of special strata
proof_stages.summary: To handle the hard boundary points with degrees (1,0) or (0,1), partial Hasse invariants/Hodge heights are introduced and used to determine precisely the possible partial degrees of all competing OF-cyclic subgroups H' in A[p].

proof_stages.label: Split U_p and convergent iteration at the hard locus
proof_stages.summary: The U_p formula is then decomposed into ordinary-safe and troublesome subgroup contributions; repeated flipping between hard boundary points leads to an infinite corrective series whose p-adic convergence is guaranteed exactly by the stated small-slope bound.

examples.name: Coleman classicality for overconvergent modular forms
examples.note: The classical modular-curve theorem and its functional-equation proof are presented as the template suggesting the same analytic-continuation strategy for inert Hilbert modular surfaces, but the later proof does not logically rely on this case.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker recalls Coleman's classicality theorem, its setup, statement, and analytic-continuation proof idea as motivation, but does not work through a concrete computation or derivation in that case.
